summary.notes: Broad serendipity scan over visible HN comment pages; history updated for surfaced items.

items.name: TinyCld
items.summary: Open-source workspace for mail, calendar, docs, drive, and apps.
items.why_included: Author comments made it clear this is a real, shipping workspace with native app and web support.
items.evidence: Nathan described TinyCld as a self-contained mail/cal/contacts/drive/text/calc suite with demo, install docs, iOS app, and repo.

items.name: HyperFormula
items.why_included: Commenters singled it out as an important dependency behind TinyCld’s spreadsheet stack.
items.evidence: A commenter called HyperFormula an amazing library and said it was the main reason TinyCld could be AGPL.

items.name: Mini Micro
items.summary: Fantasy computer and scripting environment for hobbyist programming.
items.why_included: The thread pointed to the MiniScript docs and open-source core, which makes it unusually concrete.
items.evidence: Replies discussed the MiniScript QuickRef, significant whitespace, and the open-source MiniScript portion behind Mini Micro.

items.name: Theseus
items.summary: Win32-to-WASM translator for running Windows apps on the web.
items.why_included: Commenters were excited enough to compare it with other retro-web runtimes and call out the tiny demo.
items.evidence: Replies praised the project coming back to HN and linked the live demo, GitHub, and related WASM/Windows references.

items.name: SC2K Fix
items.summary: Patch that runs SimCity 3000’s Windows 95 version on modern PCs.
items.why_included: It solves a concrete compatibility problem commenters still care about.
items.evidence: One reply said you can run the Windows 95 version on modern computers with this patch and complained GOG does not ship it.

items.name: Microlandia
items.summary: SimCity-like city builder that favors imagination over photorealism.
items.why_included: The strongest comment was a direct love letter from someone building in the same genre.
items.evidence: A commenter said they love the game so much they started making their own city builder because they dislike where the genre is going.

items.name: Metropolis 1998
items.summary: Retro city builder with modern simulation features.
items.why_included: HN commenters treated it as a promising spiritual successor to SimCity.
items.evidence: One reply said they have followed its development for some time and linked the Steam page as a modern successor worth trying.

items.name: Dorfromantik
items.source_story_title: My new obsession: A horse-racing board game of pure luck
items.summary: Cozy tile-laying board game with solo and co-op play.
items.why_included: It was repeatedly recommended as a good fit for the same light-strategy audience.
items.evidence: Commenters called it very nice, easy to learn, and mentioned the official digital adaptation and board-game versions.

items.name: Ready Set Bet
items.source_story_title: My new obsession: A horse-racing board game of pure luck
items.why_included: It came up as the more accessible recommendation in the horse-racing thread.
items.evidence: A commenter explicitly said the discussed game sounded similar to Ready Set Bet and that it is probably easier to find.

items.name: Last.fm
items.summary: Music tracking and recommendation service now independent again.
items.why_included: HN users still treat it as a sticky, high-value music history tool.
items.evidence: Commenters called it one of their favorite services and described it as the best place to track listening and recover decent recommendations.

items.name: Epicure
items.source_story_title: All of human cooking compressed into 2 megabytes
items.summary: Cooking app for browsing ingredient flavor pairings.
items.why_included: It was the concrete tool commenters used to turn the paper’s ideas into something usable.
items.evidence: A commenter said you can browse flavor combinations there and called it neat.

items.name: The Flavor Bible
items.source_story_title: All of human cooking compressed into 2 megabytes
items.summary: Cookbook and reference guide for ingredient pairings.
items.why_included: The thread surfaced it as the classic manual for cooking combinations.
items.evidence: A commenter said it was a great reference book for finding pairings of ingredients.
